model_phrasing: Model phrasing: GPT-5.x detected from the system harness; Phrasing profile id RTM-PH6OCQ6MX4.
decision_target: Preserve the smallest safe observation projection and remove the prior checkpoint evidence gap.
source_of_truth: The saved DKR-3 trace probe and contract, independent same-Scope and seam probes, independent validator record, and current Lite and observable source named by the evidence hashes
read_method: Run workers/dkr-3-v2/replay.sh and compare 12 trace dimensions, same-Scope isolation, forbidden exports, terminal settlement order, seam sufficiency, checkpoint shape, six wall entries, and all eleven named hashes.
questions_answered: The required trace covers session, activation, work, channel, role, tool, parentage, success, error, cancellation, activation close, and sink settlement at 12 of 12 dimensions.
Two overlapping activations in one Scope keep session correlation and sinks isolated with cross_session_leak_count 0.
Success and error activations each emit activation close before flush and close.
The projection exports none of the forbidden config, roots, prompts, credentials, or unknown fields.
Existing Lite wrapExec context, parent, onClose, named tag lookup, and observable runtime ownership seams are sufficient.
The public boundary is one named SDK projection tag with zero arbitrary tag enumeration paths, zero public ContextData callbacks, and zero Lite projection changes.
Every v2 evidence hash resolves to one exact named current source or worker artifact.
questions_unanswered: Public names for the SDK observation namespace and observable event context field remain an implementation naming choice.
Cancellation may extend the observable phase union or use a separate terminal field; either choice must preserve the verified terminal contract.
Activation id allocation remains a declared SDK activation edge and must be tested as activation-local unless later discovery proves durability is required.
decision: candidate only; never accepted by this worker

confidence_probability_update: 0.88 after writer probes, independently verified behavior and walls, then 0.97 after replacing the orphan hash with eleven exact named sources and completing checkpoint mechanics
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: One named safe projection tag preserves explicit composition and prevents arbitrary context export.
Same-Scope overlap proves activation-local correlation and sink isolation without a shared-scope workaround.
Existing Lite and observable seams are sufficient, so a Lite projection primitive would add unsupported surface.
The prior unresolved hash is removed rather than waived; all v2 evidence paths are exact and replayed.
Checkpoint acceptance remains independent work; this writer does not authorize downstream progression.

candidate_ckrs.target: 12/12 required trace dimensions with sensitive_context_export_count 0 and cross_session_leak_count 0
1 explicit safe projection tag, 0 arbitrary tag enumeration paths, 0 public ContextData callbacks, and 0 Lite projection changes

candidate_pkrs.scope: Define the SDK safe observation namespace and compose it at declared activation, work, channel, role, tool, and subagent execution edges.
Let the observable extension read one named projection tag, classify cancellation, and emit activation close before sink settlement.
Add same-Scope isolation, forbidden-value, parentage, terminal, close, sink settlement, and trace attribute tests.

checkpoint_rejection_policy: Superficial, tautological, false-positive, fabricated, unsupported, or single-LLM-truth checkpoints are rejected. Missing, stale, wrong-source, non-replayable, or contradicted evidence fails closed. An empty result remains valid only with a replayable probe trace.
role_grammar: CKRs are measurable contribution context, not worker work.
DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
